hard.next_step: 5/5/7 GK: Fernando said team no longer has bandwidth to engage in this project; asking for details otherwise kicking by 2-3 months
hard.ais_why: AI importance rated 'Very Critical' but no quantified business case established and the opp is early-stage; the 11 Mar demo buyer action was to disable AI note-taking per GTA policy, and on 5 May Fernando said the team no longer has bandwidth. Buyer-voice evidence pushes toward AI-Resistant. Confidence low.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal has been dark since Fernando Puerto said on 5 May 2026 the team has no bandwidth and asked to kick 2-3 months. Sourcing was found not a fit at the 11 Mar demo, scope narrowed to CLM and eProcurement, no champion advocacy, no economic buyer, and the buyer disabled AI note-taking on the call.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 11 Feb 2026 (145 days old). Close date 22 Oct 2026 (108 days out) after two rep-driven pushes (18 Jun to 29 Sep on 11 May, 29 Sep to 22 Oct on 21 May). Stage Formal Evaluation since 13 Mar 2026 (~115 days in stage, no submission-date series). LastActivityDate 11 Mar 2026. Last genuine two-way buyer exchange 5 May 2026 (62 days ago) when Fernando asked to pause 2-3 months; five rep re-engagement attempts (30 Mar, 7 Apr, 10 Apr, 24 Apr, 29 Apr) drew no reply. Cold 60+ days with the buyer having explicitly self-paused, so Off Track.
evidence: Last verified buyer engagement was the 11 Mar 2026 demo (Fernando Puerto and team); sourcing was not the right tool but iContract (CLM) and eProcurement drew interest (Next_Step__c 11 Mar 2026).
On 5 May 2026 Fernando Puerto said the team no longer has bandwidth to engage and asked to kick the project 2-3 months (Next_Step__c 5 May 2026 entry).
Five rep outreach attempts between 30 Mar and 29 Apr 2026 (calls, voicemail, email to Fernando) logged no buyer reply.
Close date was pushed twice by the rep in May (18 Jun to 29 Sep, then 29 Sep to 22 Oct) with no buyer-driven event behind either move (field history 11 May and 21 May 2026).
No economic buyer identified, no confirmed decision process, no quantified business case, and the buyer disabled AI note-taking on the 11 Mar call citing GTA policy.
Pipeline forecast on $150k is not defensible: single-threaded to a self-paused procurement director, no EB, no champion advocacy, no evaluation activity in over three months.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists. Fernando Puerto expressed 'strong fit and need' with high AI interest at the 11 Feb 2026 intro (Next_Step__c 11 Feb 2026), and the 11 Mar demo confirmed iContract (CLM) and eProcurement resonate while sourcing does not (Next_Step__c 11 Mar 2026), but the buyer never quantified cost, cycle time, contract-leakage, or headcount impact. The AIS_Why field itself flags that 'no quantified business case has yet been established'. The move is to make Fernando co-build a simple current-state cost/cycle-time baseline for contracts and eProcurement before any re-pitch.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified. The only person ever engaged is Fernando Puerto (procurement contact), who has never introduced a budget owner, IT/CIO sponsor, or the legal team he was expected to bring to the 24 Mar CLM deep dive. As a Georgia state government authority, budget and award authority almost certainly sit with an agency executive and a formal public-procurement process, but no such person appears in any field, task, or call. Identifying and reaching a funding sponsor is a top move, though it is blocked until the buyer re-engages after their self-imposed 2-3 month pause.
sources: OpportunityContactRole (only Fernando Puerto as activity contact)

decision_criteria.narrative: Partial and inferred. The 11 Mar demo established a functional cut: sourcing (iSource) is 'not quite the tool they need' while iContract (CLM) and eProcurement fit, so contract lifecycle management and eProcurement fitment plus ERP-integration readiness are the live criteria (Next_Step__c 11 Mar 2026, which also notes they 'plan to switch ERPs shortly'). AI capability was named 'Very Critical' in the opportunity data, yet the buyer's on-call action was to disable AI note-taking per GTA policy (AIS_Why, 11 Mar 2026), so AI is a stated interest but a governance-constrained one. No formal BRD or scored requirement matrix has been shared.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unmapped and stalled. The intended sequence was demo (11 Mar) then a CLM deep dive with the legal team (targeted 24 Mar), but that legal session never occurred and the buyer went quiet. Reading the call sequence itself: only Fernando has ever appeared, no legal, IT, or executive stakeholder joined, and on 5 May the buyer asked to pause the whole project 2-3 months (Next_Step__c 5 May 2026). As a public-sector authority a formal, likely competitive procurement path is probable, but none of it is confirmed. Nothing about approval chain, evaluation committee, or timeline is documented.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are entirely unmapped and premature at this early stage. No MSA, Order Form, SOW, legal-redline, or InfoSec discussion has occurred. The planned 24 Mar CLM deep dive 'with the legal team' (Next_Step__c 16 Mar 2026) would have opened the paper conversation but never happened. The buyer's planned ERP switch (Next_Step__c 11 Mar 2026) signals a future integration-scoping requirement. As a Georgia state agency, GTA will run a defined public-procurement and legal-review path; visibility only comes once the buyer re-engages and names their procurement route.

identify_pain.narrative: Confirmed qualitative pain, no quantification. The buyer needs to modernize contract lifecycle management and eProcurement with an AI angle (Next_Step__c 11 Feb and 11 Mar 2026), and is planning an ERP switch that pressures procurement-system integration (Next_Step__c 11 Mar 2026). Fernando Puerto is the pain owner. Sourcing pain is explicitly NOT present for Zycus: iSource was found 'not quite the tool they need'. The operational or financial cost of the status quo was never articulated, and the 5 May bandwidth statement suggests the pain is not yet urgent enough to fund. Deepen by quantifying contract-cycle and eProcurement cost with Fernando.

champion.narrative: Fernando Puerto is the sole engaged contact and a weak, cooling champion. He initiated the relationship on 11 Feb 2026 and ran scheduling responsively through mid-March, but did not bring the legal team to the planned 24 Mar CLM deep dive, went silent through five rep touches (30 Mar to 29 Apr), and on 5 May said the team has no bandwidth and asked to pause 2-3 months (Next_Step__c 5 May 2026). There is no evidence of advocacy: no internal introductions, no push against competitors, no agenda-driving. He is single-threaded and passive, with no confirmed access to a budget owner or decision maker.
sources: engagement pulse 62 days since last buyer touch

competition.narrative: The dominant current threat is not a named rival but no-decision inertia: the buyer self-paused the project for bandwidth reasons on 5 May 2026, so status-quo (GTA keeps its current contract and eProcurement approach and simply defers) is the most likely way this deal is lost. JAGGAER is the named competitor in Competitors__c with an unspecified 'Others', but no call, task, or Next_Step ever describes a bake-off, shortlist, or buyer preference for JAGGAER, so its current threat is unconfirmed and carried forward. Zycus's exposure is completeness: sourcing (iSource) was rejected as a fit, so a full-suite rival could frame Zycus as a partial answer. To win, re-establish urgency around CLM and eProcurement and quantify the cost of deferral.

deal_movement.summary: Momentum ran forward Feb to mid-March (intro, demo, stage moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation on 13 Mar) then reversed: buyer stopped replying through April, self-paused on 5 May, and the only May-June activity is two rep-side close-date pushes with no buyer event behind them.

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest CURRENT threat is no-decision / status quo: on 5 May 2026 Fernando Puerto asked to pause the project 2-3 months for lack of bandwidth, so the deal is most likely lost to deferral, not to a rival. JAGGAER is the only named competitor (Competitors__c) with an unspecified 'Others', but no call or activity confirms any active JAGGAER engagement, so both are carried forward at their prior threat level, not re-ranked on this zero-new-call run. Zycus's structural exposure is suite completeness after iSource was rejected as a fit on 11 Mar 2026.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage, single-threaded, buyer self-paused. The win levers are re-establishing urgency, mapping power, and reframing scope to the two modules that fit.
flags: Time the restart to the buyer's own 2-3 month pause: plan a value-led re-engage for early August around their planned ERP switch, not another chase now.
Break single-thread dependency on Fernando Puerto: identify and reach a budget owner and the legal/CLM stakeholder GTA never brought forward.
Reframe the deal to iContract (CLM) plus eProcurement where Zycus fits and drop sourcing from the pitch, so the buyer is not evaluating Zycus as a partial suite.

Scope narrowed to two modules, a defensive posture consistent with the buyer's subsequent bandwidth pause.

critical_signals.text: On 5 May 2026 Fernando Puerto said the team has no bandwidth and asked to pause the project 2-3 months, and has gone silent since.
The real threat is deferral, not a rival: the buyer self-paused and no active JAGGAER or other bake-off has ever been described.
Sourcing was rejected as a fit on 11 Mar 2026, narrowing the deal to contract management and eProcurement tied to a planned ERP switch.
No economic buyer, no quantified business case, and the close date has moved only on rep-side pushes with no buyer-backed date.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Early-stage government deal that stalled to dark: the sole contact, Fernando Puerto, asked to pause the project 2-3 months on 5 May 2026 for lack of bandwidth, sourcing was rejected as a fit, and no economic buyer or business case exists.

win_position.text: We are far from winning: the only engaged contact self-paused the project on 5 May 2026, sourcing was rejected at the 11 Mar demo, and no budget owner has ever surfaced.
The biggest risk is no-decision inertia, not a rival: GTA can simply defer CLM and eProcurement modernization indefinitely with no cost articulated.
Single-thread and governance friction compound it: Fernando disabled AI note-taking per GTA policy and never brought legal or an executive into the room.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum reversed after 13 Mar: five rep touches from 30 Mar to 29 Apr 2026 went unanswered and the buyer asked to pause on 5 May.
The only May-June movement is two rep-side close-date pushes (to 29 Sep, then 22 Oct) with no buyer-driven event behind them.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer explicitly withdrew commitment on 5 May 2026, asking to kick the project 2-3 months for lack of bandwidth.
No economic buyer, no legal/CLM stakeholder, and no quantified case: nothing binds GTA to a decision this cycle.

deal_risk.text: Highest risk is deferral: the project is self-paused with no committed restart date and a rep-inflated close of 22 Oct 2026.
Structural exposure on completeness: sourcing does not fit Zycus, so the deal is now a two-module play a full-suite rival could undercut.

champion_strength.evidence: Fernando Puerto passive and self-paused; no advocacy or access to power (Next_Step__c 5 May 2026).

customer_commitment.evidence: Buyer asked to pause 2-3 months for lack of bandwidth (Next_Step__c 5 May 2026).

deal_momentum.evidence: Five unanswered rep touches and rep-only close-date pushes; no buyer event since 11 Mar 2026.

customer_preference.evidence: No expressed preference for Zycus; buyer disabled AI note-taking and asked to pause the project 2-3 months (5 May 2026).

business_case.evidence: No quantified ROI, savings, or cycle-time impact; AIS_Why notes no business case established.

gaps: Only one buyer call on record (11 Mar 2026 demo) and only a one-line transcript fragment is available; competitive shortlist, decision process, and economic buyer never captured on a call.
No economic buyer, decision-maker, or legal/CLM stakeholder identified in any Salesforce contact role or activity.
No quantified business case anywhere across fields, calls, next steps, or tasks.
